data.title: Netflix (NFLX) Stock Has Become a Value Play Post Q2
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) stock has become a value play, in my book. The latest post-earnings sell-off pushed shares of the entertainment streaming services company to a two-year low and roughly 44% below their June 2025 peak. Wall Street saw slowing growth and shrinking transparency. These points are not entirely wrong. However, I see a company still producing double-digit revenue growth and widening margins.

In the meantime, free cash flow is set to increase significantly, which is why I believe the stock is rather cheap today. Accordingly, my NFLX forecast is bullish.
data.author: Nikolaos Sismanis

data.title: Stocks Futures Fall as Alphabet and Tesla's Earnings Spark AI Capex Concerns; Oil Prices Surge
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down on Thursday morning as the second-quarter results of tech giant Alphabet (GOOGL) and electric vehicle (EV) maker Tesla (TSLA) weighed on investor sentiment amid higher AI spending plans. Furthermore, a spike in oil prices added to investors' woes. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.93%, 0.66%, and 0.75%, respectively, at 8:25 a.m. EDT on July 23.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Oil prices surged after Tehran-backed Houthi militant group claimed attacks on two Saudi Arabian tankers in the Red Sea and President Donald Trump threatened to strike Iranian infrastructure. At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) was up 5.20% at $98.96 per barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) rose 4.91% to $90.92 per barrel.

During Wednesday's regular trading session, major U.S. stock indices closed lower ahead of Big Tech earnings and escalating geopolitical risks. The Nasdaq Composite, S&P 500, and Dow Jones fell 0.57%, 0.14%, and 0.01%, respectively, after all three had rallied in the previous session on a rebound in chip stocks.

In Thursday's pre-market trading, Alphabet and Tesla shares were down 5.1% and 7.3%, respectively. Alphabet raised its 2026 capital expenditure outlook to as much as $205 billion to expand its AI infrastructure. Meanwhile, Tesla outlined plans for higher capital spending as it continues investing in AI, Optimus robots, and Robotaxis.

In contrast, ServiceNow (NOW) stock rose more than 6% as the enterprise software company delivered market-beating Q2 earnings, easing AI disruption concerns. Also, Lockheed Martin (LMT) was up more than 5% after the defense company reported upbeat Q2 results.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Musk Calls Optimus 'Biggest Product Ever' as Tesla Stock Drops after Q2 Earnings Miss
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) stock slipped after the company reported weaker-than-expected second-quarter earnings. Despite the miss, CEO Elon Musk remained optimistic, calling the Optimus humanoid robot Tesla's "biggest product ever." He reaffirmed the company's long-term vision for Optimus but stated that there is still "a lot of work" ahead before the robot can be produced at scale. TSLA stock is down by 6.10% in pre-market hours on Thursday.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Explore TSLQ for 2X short leverage on TSLA

For context, Tesla posted earnings of $0.33 per share, missing Wall Street's estimate of $0.53. However, revenue came in at $28.24 billion, topping analysts' expectations of $26.40 billion for Q2.

Big Hopes, Big Hurdles for Tesla's Optimus

During the second-quarter earnings call, Musk told investors that scaling Optimus will be one of Tesla's biggest manufacturing challenges. He said the company needs to build almost the entire supply chain from scratch because nearly every component in the robot is new.

Despite the challenges, Tesla said Optimus production remains on track to begin later this year. This is a faster timeline than many investors expected. He added that the first Optimus robots will be used inside Tesla to gather training data and improve the robot before a wider rollout.

Additionally, Tesla provided an update on its Robotaxi expansion, saying the service is now active across seven major metro areas. Musk said Robotaxi mileage is growing by more than 10% each week. However, he noted that the rollout will continue to prioritize safety, which could limit the pace of expansion.

Tesla's AI Spending

Tesla continues to invest aggressively in its long-term AI ambitions, including Optimus and Robotaxis. CFO Vaibhav Taneja reaffirmed that capital expenditures will exceed $25 billion in fiscal 2026 and are expected to keep rising over the next two to three years.

While these investments could fuel future growth, they are also weighing on near-term cash flow. In the second quarter, Tesla reported negative free cash flow of $1.1 billion, highlighting the high cost of funding its AI ambitions.

Is Tesla a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, TSLA stock has received a Hold consensus rating, with eight Buys, 16 Holds, and three Sells assigned in the last three months. The average price target for Tesla shares is $397.05, suggesting a potential upside of 6.16% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: ServiceNow Stock (NOW) Rises 5% after Q2 Earnings — Can the Rally Continue?
data.description:
ServiceNow (NOW) stock rose over 5% in Thursday's pre-market trading after the software company reported better-than-expected second-quarter results. Revenue rose 24% year-over-year to $3.98 billion, beating estimates of $3.93 billion. Meanwhile, adjusted earnings of $0.90 per share also topped the $0.86 estimate. The results suggest NOW stock could have more room to rise, helped by growing AI sales and steady customer demand.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Following the results, CEO Bill McDermott struck a confident tone. He said ServiceNow is "firing on all cylinders" and noted its 98% renewal rate. Contract terms are also getting longer. McDermott expects greater AI use to bring more work and higher volume to ServiceNow's platform.

AI Is Becoming a Bigger Business for ServiceNow

ServiceNow raised its 2026 AI annual contract value target by 50% to $1.5 billion. The increase comes as more customers adopt its AI products.

This is a good sign for a company that has faced fears about AI disrupting traditional software. Instead, ServiceNow is finding ways to make money from the shift.

One opportunity comes from the growing use of AI agents. Companies need ways to manage these agents and connect them with daily tasks. ServiceNow wants its platform to play that role.

Cybersecurity Adds Another Source of Growth

ServiceNow is also expanding in cybersecurity. McDermott said the company now has a "10-figure cybersecurity business." He also said the business is growing faster than other large cybersecurity firms.

ServiceNow's contracted business also remains healthy. Its current remaining performance obligations (cRPO), which measures contracted revenue expected within the next 12 months, reached $13.2 billion. The figure grew 21.5% year-over-year in constant currency.

JPMorgan Flags One Concern

Despite the solid quarter, JPMorgan analyst Mark Murphy sees one area of concern. He pointed to a slowdown in organic constant-currency cRPO growth.

Murphy also noted that the higher AI target led to only a modest increase in ServiceNow's overall revenue outlook. Investors may want to see more of that AI demand show up in the company's broader growth over time.

Still, Murphy maintained an Overweight rating on NOW stock. He lowered his price target to $145 from $195 but believes the recent stock weakness already reflects much of the slower growth outlook.

What Is the Target Price for NOW Stock?

On TipRanks, the average ServiceNow price target of $140.21 implies 46.88% upside potential from current levels. NOW stock carries a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 29 Buys, two Holds, and one Sell rating.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 7/23/26 – Futures Fall as AI Capex Concerns, Iran Tensions Weigh on Markets
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell ahead of Thursday's session as investors weighed Alphabet's (GOOGL) and Tesla's (TSLA) higher AI spending plans. Rising Treasury yields on renewed inflation concerns and higher oil prices also pressured market sentiment.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.37%, 0.38%, and 0.41%, respectively, at 5:52 a.m. EDT on July 23.

Alphabet and Tesla shares were trending lower in premarket trading, extending their declines from the previous session. Alphabet raised its 2026 capital expenditure outlook to as much as $205 billion to expand its AI infrastructure. Meanwhile, Tesla outlined plans for higher capital spending as it continues investing in AI, Optimus robots, and Robotaxis. The higher spending plan renewed investor concerns about whether massive AI investments by hyperscalers will generate sufficient returns.

Oil prices rose on Thursday after a tanker was hit by an unidentified projectile off the coast of Saudi Arabia and President Donald Trump threatened to strike Iranian infrastructure. The latest escalation followed Trump's warning that the U.S. would target Iranian bridges or power plants if Tehran continued attacking ships in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran responded by threatening retaliation against U.S.-linked infrastructure and energy facilities across the region.

At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) was up 3.26% at $95.60 a barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) rose 4.31% to $90.65 a barrel.

During Wednesday's regular trading session, major U.S. stock indices closed lower as investors awaited earnings from several Big Tech companies after the closing bell. The Nasdaq, S&P 500, and Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.57%, 0.14%, and 0.01%, respectively, after all three had rallied in the previous session on a rebound in chip stocks.

The benchmark U.S. 10-year Treasury yield stood at 4.67% as of the last check. Additionally, the spot gold price in U.S. dollars traded around $4,090.51 per ounce on Thursday.

On the earnings front, chip giant Intel (INTC) is set to report earnings, along with Lockheed Martin (LMT), American Airlines (AAL), T-Mobile US (TMUS), and Comcast (CMCSA).

Elsewhere, European markets remained in the red as traders awaited the European Central Bank's interest-rate decision, with most investors expecting policymakers to keep rates unchanged.

Asia-Pacific Markets Close Higher

Asia-Pacific markets bucked the broader trend and ended mostly higher. South Korean memory-chip maker SK Hynix (SKHY) said it had capped the number of locally listed shares that could be converted into U.S. American depositary receipts at 2.5% of its total outstanding shares.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index surged 1.28%. In China, the Shanghai Composite gained 0.25%, while the Shenzhen Component rose 0.49%. In Japan, the Nikkei gained 0.46% while the Topix added 0.51%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: QuantumScape Stock (QS) Falls 5% after Q2 Earnings — What Spooked Investors?
data.description:
QuantumScape (QS) stock fell about 5% in pre-market trading on Thursday after the solid-state battery maker reported its second-quarter results. The company posted a loss of $0.16 per share, narrower than the $0.18 loss expected by Wall Street. While QuantumScape reported a smaller-than-expected loss, its commercial rollout is still at an early stage. Investors are waiting to see when its progress with customers will lead to larger-scale production and sales.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Investors may also be weighing a change to QuantumScape's deal with Volkswagen's (VWAGY) PowerCo. Under the revised agreement, potential milestone payments fell from about $131 million to $75 million. However, the company said related project costs will also fall, making the expected cash impact roughly neutral.

Q2 Highlights Beyond Headline Numbers

QuantumScape Expands Its Automaker Deals

During the earnings update, QuantumScape highlighted its growing work with major automakers. The company recently signed a multi-year deal with Honda (HMC) to advance its solid-state battery technology. QuantumScape said the deal followed a "rigorous" review of its technology.

The company also updated its licensing deal with Volkswagen's (VWAGY) PowerCo. In addition, it is working with two other top-10 automakers. QuantumScape recently shipped cells to another major carmaker for testing.

With more automakers testing its batteries, QuantumScape now needs to show it can make more cells to meet this demand.

QuantumScape Plans to Double Cell Output

On that front, management reported progress at its Eagle Line pilot facility. Its main tools are running with uptime above 90%. QuantumScape now plans to double cell output in the second half of 2026.

There are also early signs of more business activity. Customer billings reached $10.8 million in Q2 and $21.8 million in the first half. That already tops the $19.5 million recorded for all of 2025.

The higher output should help QuantumScape send more cells to customers. It could also speed up testing as the company works toward wider use of its batteries.

What Comes Next for QS Stock?

QuantumScape ended Q2 with about $859 million in liquidity. It also lowered its 2026 capital spending forecast to between $27 million and $37 million. Its adjusted EBITDA loss forecast remains at $250 million to $275 million.

Investors will now watch whether higher cell output leads to more customer deals and sales. Progress at the Eagle Line could help QuantumScape move from battery testing toward larger-scale production.

Is QS Stock a Strong Buy? 

According to the TipRanks AI Analyst model, QS stock received a score of 45 out of 100 with a Neutral rating. Further, the AI analyst has set a price target of $5.50 on QuantumScape stock, which implies a downside of nearly 6%.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: ServiceNow Stock Price Forecast — What Financial Analysts Are Saying after NOW's Q2 Earnings
data.description:
ServiceNow (NOW) stock rose more than 6% in Thursday's pre-market trading after the software company reported better-than-expected Q2 results. The results were helped by solid demand for its software and growing AI sales. Following the report, several Wall Street analysts remained bullish on NOW stock. Here's what financial analysts expect from ServiceNow after its latest earnings report.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

For context, ServiceNow provides software that helps companies manage IT tasks and other business processes. Q2 revenue rose 24% year-over-year to $3.98 billion, beating the $3.93 billion estimate. Adjusted earnings of $0.90 per share also topped the $0.86 forecast. The company also raised its 2026 AI annual contract value target by 50% to $1.5 billion.

Latest Analysts' Views on NOW Stock

Looking ahead, Wall Street analysts remain positive on ServiceNow stock. In a new report today, BMO analyst Keith Bachman raised his price target on NOW stock to $118 from $115 and maintained an Outperform rating. He noted that cRPO, or contracted revenue expected within the next 12 months, beat forecasts, though part of the upside came from deals closing earlier than planned. Looking ahead, Bachman expects underlying cRPO growth to remain roughly steady in Q3. He also sees ServiceNow's valuation as attractive compared with its growth.

Meanwhile, Oppenheimer analyst Brian Schwartz maintained an Outperform rating and a $140 price target. He said most of ServiceNow's Q2 measures beat forecasts and pointed to strong AI demand.

Schwartz expects the company's AI business to exceed its $1.5 billion ACV target for 2026. He also sees room for growth in cybersecurity, which has become a $1 billion-plus business. However, he noted that the size of ServiceNow's recent earnings beats has become smaller and growth continues to slow.

Likewise, Citizens analyst Patrick Walravens maintained a Market Outperform rating and a $157 price target. He pointed to solid Q2 revenue, subscription sales, and cRPO growth. However, ServiceNow's gross margin came in at 77.9%, below the 79.2% expected by Wall Street.

What Is the Target Price for NOW Stock?

On TipRanks, the average ServiceNow price target of $140.21 implies 46.88% upside potential from current levels. NOW stock carries a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 29 Buys, two Holds, and one Sell rating.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Tesla Stock Slumps as Analysts Cut Price Targets on Higher Capex Outlook
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) stock extended its losses in premarket trading after the EV maker reported mixed second-quarter results and outlined higher capital spending plans. Several Wall Street analysts also lowered their price targets following the report. Morgan Stanley analyst Andrew Percoco cut his price target on TSLA from $417 to $400 (6.9% upside), while maintaining his Hold rating. Similarly, Mizuho Securities analyst Vijay Rakesh kept his Buy rating but lowered his price target from $480 to $460 (23% upside).  

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Explore TSLQ for 2X short leverage on TSLA

Tesla reported adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $0.33 for the second quarter, below analysts' expectations of $0.53. Revenue increased 26% year-over-year to $28.24 billion, topping Wall Street's estimate of $26.40 billion. The company said expenses grew faster than revenue due to continued investments in AI and manufacturing.

Here's Why Tesla's Capex Is Rising

Meanwhile, the company said that it expects capital expenditures to exceed $25 billion this year and remain elevated over the next two to three years. Tesla plans to invest heavily in expanding its Robotaxi fleet, increasing Optimus robot production, building semiconductor and solar manufacturing capacity, and growing its AI computing infrastructure. To help fund these investments, Tesla is putting debt facilities in place that could provide access to as much as $30 billion.

Percoco Sees $30B in Capex Next Year

Percoco said investors largely expected higher capital spending, but the updated outlook suggests that spending will remain higher for several years, leading to greater cash burn through the end of the decade. He believes these investments are necessary for Tesla to maintain its leadership in autonomous driving and robotics. However, he said investors will want proof that the spending is paying off, particularly through the expansion of its Robotaxi network and the commercial launch of Optimus.

He also expects Tesla's automotive and energy margins to remain under pressure. Percoco cut his adjusted EBITDA forecasts by 7% for 2026 and 12% for 2027. He now expects Tesla to spend nearly $30 billion on capital expenditures in 2027, up from his previous estimate of $20 billion. As a result, he estimates free cash flow burn of about $14 billion, compared with his earlier forecast of $5 billion.

Rakesh Is Optimistic about Tesla's AI Strategy

Rakesh said Tesla delivered a stronger-than-expected quarter, with vehicle deliveries rising 25% year-over-year, outperforming other EV makers. He also remains optimistic about Tesla's long-term AI strategy.

Rakesh expects Tesla to launch Full Self-Driving (FSD) Version 15 in the second half of 2026 and noted that 55% of new Tesla buyers in North America now purchase the FSD package. Active FSD subscriptions also increased 16% quarter-over-quarter to about 1.48 million.

He added that Cybercab production has begun at Gigafactory Texas, with Tesla's Robotaxi fleet logging more than 380,000 driverless miles across seven cities without any major incidents. However, Tesla's robotaxi fleet remains much smaller than Alphabet (GOOGL) Waymo's. Rakesh also expects Optimus humanoid robot production to begin soon, ahead of a planned commercial launch in 2027. While higher AI investments will keep capital spending elevated, he expects Tesla to deliver 1.79 million vehicles in 2026, above Wall Street's forecast.

Is TSLA Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

On TipRanks, TSLA has a Hold consensus rating based on eight Buys, 16 Holds, and three Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $397.05 implies 6.2% upside potential from current levels. Year-to-date, TSLA shares have dropped 16.8%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Reddit Stock Sinks on News of Google Rift – Here's What Analysts Make of It
data.description:
Wednesday was a bad day for Reddit (NYSE:RDDT) stock. Shares fell by 8% after The Wall Street Journal reported that the company may restrict Google parent Alphabet's access to its content for AI-related purposes as a $60 million-per-year licensing agreement approaches expiration.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The report said Reddit is weighing whether to limit Google's access to its platform for AI training or seek a substantially higher renewal fee. Other publishers, including USA Today, Reuters, and Politico, are likewise reassessing Google's web crawler as AI-generated summaries reduce the traffic sent to their websites. The Wall Street Journal attributed its report to people familiar with the matter, while Reddit has not publicly commented.

The development follows a Digiday report last week that cited an executive discussing a similar possibility and broadly aligns with RBC analyst Brad Erickson's checks, which suggested that Reddit was being pushed to introduce content and account-quality controls to better accommodate Google's core and spam updates in May and June, respectively.

On the whole, Erickson views the situation as negative for Reddit, arguing that investor expectations had risen in recent months that the company would secure a favourable outcome with Google and potentially announce it at an upcoming earnings report, making this a "significant step backwards." The analyst says content quality has long been a challenge, with the rapid expansion of AI-generated content making the issue more difficult as Google continues to evolve its algorithms and AI content standards.

While Reddit retains leverage through its leading share of citations in AI Overviews, Erickson notes that the company has been seeking a variable compensation arrangement or increased traffic rather than simply renewing its existing fixed $60 million annual payment, while Google has strong incentives to limit content-cost inflation and recognises its significant influence over Reddit's traffic and valuation.

Ultimately, the analyst believes the uncertainty surrounding Google's long-term AI strategy makes it difficult to negotiate guarantees around future traffic, as even Google might not yet know how its AI search and broader internet strategy will evolve.

All told, Erickson maintained a Sector Perform (i.e., Neutral) rating on the shares, although that might as well be a Buy, given his $250 price target suggests shares will climb 47% higher in the year ahead. (To watch Erickson's track record, click here)

D.A. Davidson analyst Wyatt Swanson also believes the WSJ report is significant given investors had expected details of Reddit's Google partnership renewal to emerge in the coming quarters. At the same time, recent traffic deceleration and Reddit's apparent testing of measures to push mobile web users toward its app suggest the company may be preparing to reduce its reliance on Google-driven referral traffic.

Although this could pose a risk to future DAUq (Daily Active Uniques) growth, particularly among logged-out users, the analyst does not believe investors should assume the partnership will simply end, noting that both companies have incentives to find a way to preserve referral traffic while accommodating Google's AI search.

Swanson also argues that Reddit's largely human-generated content gives it meaningful leverage in negotiations, as its data is particularly valuable to AI models, while the company could potentially offset weaker search-driven traffic by increasing direct user engagement.

All told, Swanson maintained a Buy rating on the shares, while his $200 price target implies the stock will post growth of 17% over the one-year timeframe. (To watch Swanson's track record, click here)

Elsewhere on the Street, the stock claims an additional 12 Buys, 6 Holds and 1 Sell, for a Moderate Buy consensus view. The forecast calls for 12-month returns of 30%, considering the average target clocks in at $222. (See RDDT stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Comcast Beat Its Earnings Goals. So Will Media Spinoffs Fix Its Broadband Problem?
data.description:
Comcast stock (CMCSA) rose in early morning trading after quarterly profits topped Wall Street estimates, but spinning off its media assets won't automatically solve its underlying broadband customer loss. While splitting off its media assets makes the business simpler, Comcast still has to compete with phone companies and satellite internet services to keep users.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

CMCSA shares climbed 2.5% to $24.10 before the opening bell, rebounding from recent low levels. The gain came as adjusted second-quarter earnings reached $1.04 per share, topping analyst expectations of 97 cents per share. Total revenue reached $29.9 billion, which also came in higher than the $29.2 billion that Wall Street projected.

Comcast Topped Its Quarterly Financial Estimates

The solid earnings report gave investors a reason to buy shares after a long period of weak stock performance. Over the past year, Comcast's stock has dropped nearly 30% as competitors continue to challenge its internet operations.

Telephone companies like Verizon (VZ), AT&T (T), and T-Mobile (TMUS) have been pulling customers away with wireless home internet services. Satellite services from companies like SpaceX have also added extra choices for rural users, putting direct pressure on Comcast's traditional service footprint.

Broadband Losses Are Troubling Comcast

Even with the positive profit figures, the company's broadband division continues to lose users. Comcast lost 167,000 residential internet customers during the three-month period, slightly higher than the 165,300 subscriber losses analysts had expected.

The loss of internet users remains a primary concern for investors because fast broadband connections serve as the main source of cash flow for the entire company. Keeping its main internet customers is vital for Comcast's long-term success, even if its movie studios do well.

Media Spinoff Could Help Comcast

To help ease these business pressures, Comcast plans to split into two separate companies over the next year. The company intends to spin off NBCUniversal and Sky into a stand-alone media firm while keeping its core broadband and wireless services.

Wall Street analysts view this structural change as a positive move that could help unlock hidden value in the stock. 5-star analyst Bryan Kraft at Deutsche Bank (DB) upgraded the stock to Buy with a $32 price target. Kraft explained that this move will help "unlock valuation upside by forcing the market to value NBCU and Comcast separately, instead of assigning a (lower) cable multiple to the entire enterprise."

Kraft added that his team sees "~30% upside over the next 12 months based on this value unlock." Though management said no deal is planned right away, he explained that splitting into two independent companies gives Comcast the freedom to jump on future deals.

Is Comcast Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on CMCSA stock based on six Buys, 10 Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. The average 12-month CMCSA price target of $31.52 per share implies 34% upside potential. (See CMCSA stock forecast)
data.author: Annika Masrani
